name: Serra Gelada Loop from Villajoyosa
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada — Vuelta desde La Villajoyosa
description: Cycle 31.5 miles (50.6 km) through Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada, enjoying moderate climbs and panoramic Mediterranean views.
region_intro: Road cycling around La Vila Joiosa offers a diverse landscape, combining coastal beauty with challenging mountainous terrain. The region, situated on the Costa Blanca, features well-paved roads, moderate climbs, and panoramic Mediterranean views. Cyclists can explore routes around the Amadorio Reservoir, through natural parks, and into the mountainous interior, encountering varied elevation gains. The area is characterized by its distinct blend of seaside roads and inland hills. Best road cycling routes around La Vila Joiosa The most popular road cycling route is Serra Gelada Loop from Villajoyosa, a 31.5 miles (50.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain and stunning views of the natural park and the Mediterranean. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Amadorio Reservoir – View of Finestrat loop from la Vila Joiosa, a moderate 23.7 miles (38.1 km) path. This route provides spectacular views of the turquoise waters of the reservoir reflecting the surrounding mountains. Local road cyclists also love the L' Almiserá Monument – The Hanging Houses of La Vila Joiosa loop from la Vila Joiosa, a 26.9 miles (43.2 km) trail leading through charming historical sites and past the iconic multi-colored fishermen's houses, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Road cycling around La Vila Joiosa is defined by coastal roads, inland hills, and routes around reservoirs. The network offers options for easy rides along promenades as well as more challenging routes with considerable elevation changes. The routes in La Vila Joiosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 985 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Vila Joiosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in La Vila Joiosa?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around La Vila Joiosa listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes along the coast, around reservoirs, and into the more mountainous interior.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around La Vila Joiosa?
A: La Vila Joiosa offers a varied terrain for road cyclists. You'll find well-paved roads along the beautiful coastline, moderate climbs around natural features like the Amadorio Reservoir, and more challenging ascents into the mountainous interior towards areas like Relleu and Callosa de Segura. The region combines stunning Mediterranean views with diverse landscapes.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in La Vila Joiosa?
A: The warm climate of the Costa Blanca makes La Vila Joiosa an appealing destination for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides, while winter can also be pleasant. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, La Vila Joiosa offers several easy road cycling routes. For a relaxed ride with minimal elevation change, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poniente Promenade – La Villajoyosa loop from la Vila Joiosa&lt;/a&gt;. This route is ideal for combining sun, sea, and exercise, making it suitable for beginners or a family outing.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking more demanding rides, the region's mountainous interior provides considerable elevation changes. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891025410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Gelada Loop from Villajoyosa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 50 kilometers with significant elevation gain, offering stunning views of the natural park and the Mediterranean.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I can see along the routes?
A: Road cycling routes in La Vila Joiosa often feature spectacular views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a significant draw, offering turquoise waters reflecting the surrounding mountains. You can also enjoy panoramic Mediterranean views, especially from routes that traverse the Serra Gelada Natural Park or coastal roads. The region's diverse coastline with its numerous coves and rocky cliffs also provides beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around La Vila Joiosa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38262211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir – View of Finestrat loop from la Vila Joiosa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31701978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L' Almiserá Monument – The Hanging Houses of La Vila Joiosa loop from la Vila Joiosa&lt;/a&gt;, which both offer circular paths.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I explore while cycling?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can cycle past charming historical sites. The iconic multi-colored fishermen's houses on the seafront of La Vila Joiosa are a visual highlight. The historic walled Old Town, with its narrow cobblestone streets and Renaissance walls, is also an Asset of Cultural Interest. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31701978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L' Almiserá Monument – The Hanging Houses of La Vila Joiosa loop from la Vila Joiosa&lt;/a&gt; incorporate some of these charming features.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in La Vila Joiosa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, combining coastal beauty with challenging mountainous terrain, and the well-paved roads. The spectacular views of the Mediterranean and natural features like the Amadorio Reservoir are frequently highlighted as favorite aspects.

Q: Are there routes that pass by the Amadorio Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a popular feature for cyclists in the region. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38262211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir – View of Finestrat loop from la Vila Joiosa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir – Bridge over the Sella River loop from la Vila Joiosa&lt;/a&gt; offer spectacular views of its turquoise waters and the surrounding mountains.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on road cycling routes in La Vila Joiosa?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly depending on the route. Flatter coastal rides, like the Poniente Promenade loop, have minimal elevation. However, routes extending inland or through natural parks can feature considerable climbs. For example, the Serra Gelada Loop includes over 770 meters of ascent, while routes around the Amadorio Reservoir typically have around 500-600 meters of elevation gain.
tips: One of the icons of Benidorm where you will have the best panoramic views. The road can be a bit tedious due to traffic especially in season but when you get there you will be rewarded with some amazing views!

One of the highest points of the Serra Gelada Park which you can reach by bike and have amazing views towards Altea and Calpe. It is recommended to go with caution since the path is shared and pedestrians have priority, especially in season when it can be quite crowded.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate road ride. It covers approximately 50.6 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 780 meters. While there are continuous ups and downs and some challenging sections, it is generally manageable for cyclists with a good level of fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily well-paved, making it suitable for road bikes. You can expect undulating surfaces with continuous ups and downs, typical of a coastal mountain range. The route offers a smooth ride for road cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of views and scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and the dramatic cliffs of the Serra Gelada Natural Park. The route also offers glimpses of the Benidorm skyline, pine forests, and on clear days, views extending to the Peñón de Ifach in Calpe and Benidorm Island.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or highlights on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by significant landmarks. You'll see The Cross of Benidorm and the Albir Lighthouse, both offering excellent viewpoints within the natural park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Costa Blanca region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less intense sun than the peak summer months. Winter can also be good, though some days might be cooler or windier.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route through the Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada. The park is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route from La Villajoyosa?
faqs.answer: While specific parking information for the exact trailhead isn't provided, La Villajoyosa offers various public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking near the town center or along the coast, keeping in mind that it's a popular area.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, and while dogs are generally allowed in the natural park, bringing a dog on a road bike can be challenging. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always keep it on a leash when off the bike, respecting park regulations.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the route in La Villajoyosa?
faqs.answer: Yes, La Villajoyosa is a vibrant coastal town with numerous cafes, restaurants, and pubs, especially along its promenade and in the town center. You'll find plenty of options to refuel before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Where can I rent a bike in La Villajoyosa?
faqs.answer: La Villajoyosa, being a popular tourist destination, has several bike rental shops. A quick local search or inquiry at your accommodation should provide options for renting road bikes suitable for this route.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including El Camí IT5TR1 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Camino del Sureste - Ramal de Benidorm, and El Camí IT5TR2 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana.
